Bellarmine he self do tell we , the Jew do fast on that day in contempt of it . and when the manichee fast upon this day , say a Jesuit ( howbeit false , for the manichee spring up long after ) but when the Jew in deed fast upon this day : the Christians , to be unlike unto they , appoint the contrary : nefas est sabbato ieiunare , as speak Tertullian . and that it be the Jew which the church herein avoid : it be plain , first by Ignatius word which allude to they : ieiunare in sabbato , est Christum occidere : and then by the reason of after time , which bend they self in this custom against all jewish contempt of this day . afterward the manichee fast upon it : and now to avoid all likeness with they , qui ieiunaverit die Dominica sicut Manichaei , anathema sit . so Augustine affirm , that since the time that the manichee appoint that day for fast , it be a fearful and a horrible thing for the christian to fast like they upon it . this conformity be the more horrible , the more earnest a man be in it : ( as man now be eager in cross , and surplice ) in like manner to conform with papist , qui Dominico die studiose ieiunat , non creditur esse Catholicus . the like care have the primitive church to avoid the Saturday fast , because it do conform with Marcion , qui sabbato ieiunavit in odium dei Creatoris omnium . before this fast of martion , the Christians of the west do use to fast upon the Saturday : after he once arise , this fast be forbid to a lay man upon pain of excommunication ; to a cleric upon pain of deposition by the sixtiefifth canon of the apostle , which be not devise till now , even in the judgement of Baronius . if they leave a ancient fast , when once heretic do abuse it , shall not we leave a ancient cross , now the papist do abuse it ? the like care we see take , to avoid likeness with the Jew in the celebration of their passcouer , or any other of their festivity . This be find in one of the epistle of Ignatius , si quis cum Iudaeis celebrate Pascha , aut Symbola festiuitatis eorum recipit ▪ particeps est eorum qui dominum occiderunt , and apostolos eius . the council of nice do after appoint a diverse day upon this reason : there aught nothing to be common between the Jew and the Christians . the canon of the apostle of Christ excommunicate he , that folemnize his easter upon the fourteenth day of the month like to the Jew . and so do the councille . a contrary day to make unlike unto the Jew be think to be fit and convenient by all . in so much that the Audaeani and Quartadecimani be censure for heretic , that do concur in the same day with they . when certain country in the east do the like , how do Constantine take on against they ? further yet : do the Jew rest on a Saturday ? the christian that do the like , be excommunicate he may on the Saturday meditate ; there may be a assembly upon that day , but he that rest upon that day iudaize , and be not to be suffer . we read amongst Iulians policy for the subversion of Christianity , this to be one : that he set on the Jew to set up their temple and the sabbath service thereof , partly to disgrace the christian service , which use not that outward splendencie in their ceremony to please the eye ; and partly to grace the ethnic worship which concord with the jewish in sacrifice and in diverse other rite . i add this to the former , that by the comparison of contrariety , it may the better appear unto we how necessary a duty it be to make our sabbath a distinction between our self and the paniste : let our sabbath , our feast , and the rite and ceremony of they be different , and we trace the step of the primitive church , and keep our faith in purity , let they be like , and then we disgrace our sister church that be reform , but grace the antichristian synagogue with who we choose to concur , then to concord with they . as if a cross or a surplice ( say our opposite ) be such matter of importance to breed concordance or concurrence ? Deut. 20.16.17.18 . . and God denounce irreconciliable war against the amalekite , and he charge his people with perpetual enmity against they Exod. 17. . that kind of war ( say Augustine be without all doubt just , which God do command , with who there be no iniquity , and who know what ought to be do to every man , in which action the army be not so much to be account the author of war , as the minister thereof August . in Ios. quoth . 10. . and so the prophet Esaie say , that it be not necessary for king Ezechias to answer any thing to the ambassador of the philistine of the Israelite right in Palestine , but only this , that God will have that land to be his people Esai . 15. : nay , the very heathen as the aethiopian do undertake every war by the oracle of Jupiter Herodot . lib . 2. . and the spartan by lot and miracle , be move to make war and to fight with the argive Xenoph. 4. Graec. . And Aeneas come into Italy to maintain war by destiny , and oracle Nat. come . 6. 14. . and the Turk do always pretend this cause of their war , that it be the commandment of Mahomet , that they shall persecute man of diverse religion : therefore they and the persian , the one seem heretical to the other , be in continual war . and the late king of Spain Phillip , do pretend this defence of his war ( as some testify ) that they be against infidel , and heretic Ferrat . de inimic . § . 7. et 17. . yet a doctor of his own sect Baltasar Ayala think that war be not to be levy against infidel , because they be infidel , although the emperor or pope shall command it : for their infidelity do not deprive they of these demesne , Baltas . Ayal . lib . 1. de iur . bel . c . 2. which they have by the law of nation : for the earth be not give to the faithful only , but to every reasonable creature : for the earth be the lord , and the fullness thereof , the round world and whosoever dwell therein Genes . c . 1. Exod. 9.29 . Psal. 24.1 . . and the lord make his sun to shine both upon the good and the bad Math. 5. in fin . et c . 6. in princip . , and though Nabuchadnezer be a infidel , yet the lord do give unto he kingdom and principality Ierem. 27.6 . . but in ground war upon divine cause , it be good to be certain of God his will , and not to credit the equivocal prophecy and fantasy of man lighthead and possess of fiery spirit , fit to kindle tumult and uproar : for the warrant of such man be nothing else , but the wrack of a number of man . such be the oracle of that scot unto his king , consult with he of war against England , ibis , redibis , nunquam in bello moriturus , which fall out afterward to be true , be thus distinguish : ibis , redibis nunquam in bello moriturus . such be the warrant of the eremit , move the imperial army to fight against the ligurian carol . Sigon in vit . Andr. Anti. . such be the fond prophecy of ball , or accord to some chronicle wall a priest , who stir up a rebellious army in the time of king Richard the second i . stow in Richard 2. . but from war which display the banner , i will pass to league which wrap it up : 2. as war have be by the law of nation maintain , so league have be conclude : for as Cicero say : ita bellum suscipiatur ut nihil aliud quàm pax quaesita videatur Cicer. 1. office . . such be the opinion of saint Augustine , as appear by the canon law : pacem habere debet voluntas , bellum necessitas : non enim pax quaeritur , ut bellum excitetur , sed bellum geritur ut pax acquiratur C. 3. 23. q . 1. . and that be expedient for the conqueror , accord to the say of Euripides : pacem reduci velle victoria expedit , victo necesse est . but the conqueror ought to be of that power , that he may be able to make perpetual peace : for it be one of the natural property of peace to be perpetual : for such be the form of peace , which the roman conclude : Romanis and Latinorum populis pax esto , dum coelum et terra stationem eandem obtinent Dionys. Halicarnas . lib 6. . and so the roman emperor , and the persian king do establish peace sine termino Procop. 1. lib . de bel . Pers. , in conclude peace , public profit must be especial regard ; which Hannibal therefore term vinculum maximum Liui. 36. : and before he Demosthenes term it so Demosth. and Ep. Phi. . and sometime private profit be respect , when it be a mean or way to public profit : wherefore duaren say pleasant and fit : we see very often , that as of a comedy , so of a war , the final conclusion be a marriage Duar . c . 3. the rit . nup . : but it be good to make peace , so that there be no fear of future tumult , and unquietness : therefore Lentulus his counsel be good against the carthaginian : quoniam perfidiam non possumus tollere , ante omnia debilitemus potentiam Appia . in bel . punic . . And Cato of this matter speak to the Spaniard : id ne fiat vno modo caueri potest , si effectum erit ne possitis rebellare Liui. 34. . And Iphicrates do well answer the lacedaemonian , promise all faithfulness , and all possible security , that he can not rest upon any other faith , or other security than this , that it shall be apparent unto he , that they can not do any hurt , though they will . the reason of this sure and wary deal with enemy , saint Jerome witty show : quis unquam mortalium iuxta viperam securos somnos capit , quae etsi non percutiat , certè sollicitat : Securius igitur est perire non posse , quàm iuxta periculum non perijsse Hieron . ep . 47. : who do ever rest quiet , sleep near to a viper , which though she do not sting , yet she do vex ? it be therefore more secure to be able to avoid danger , than not to have perish , where there be hazard of perish . the effect of peace conclude be diverse , for either weapon be lay aside , or the party agree upon condition , or the fight receive some restreint , or limitation : for when both party be weary , both of pain and of expense , this be rather a end of war , than a conclude of peace ; as Tacitus say in the like case : Bellum magis desierat quàm pax caeperat Tac. 4. annal . . and the like matter Cicero , in the like sort censure : summum otium sed senescentis magis ciuitatis quàm quiescentis Cic. ep . ad queen fr . lib . 2. ep . 14. : wherefore it be good to make and ratify perseverance of peace , because the relic of the disease after the crisis do many time work the ruin and subversion of our health . And accord to the rule of physic : the ash of putrify body will soon inflame the humour Hippocr . 6. epid . 2. et Merc. ad 2. de mor . : and therefore Tullus that artificial warrior , in articulate peace with the Albanes , put they in mind , that they ought not so much to settle present peace , as to provide for future agreement Dionys. 3. . and worthy do Isocrates reprove the grecian , because they do not compound , but delay enmity , until such time as one of they may destroy a other Isoc . in panegyr . . and excellent be that say of Cicero : pax est non in armis positis , at in abiecto omni armorum metu Cic. 10. lib . epist . Iam. epist . 6. . but to speak some what of the diversity of make peace , if this clause be comprehend in the article , that one of the party shall preserve the majesty and authority of the other with all kindness and faithfulness . doctor Baldus term this simplicem adhaerentiam Bal. 5. con . 106. , whereby ( as romanus expound it ) the weak part do adhere to the mighty : non ut subditi fiunt , sed ut defendantur Rom. con . 417. . this adherence be less than protection : and he that be a herent , or under protection , be not present under jurisdiction , but be only defensible from injury and violence , and that by soldier and armour : wherefore i wonder at alciat , when he say , Faederati latin loquentibus subditi sunt , non sotij Alcia . 7. con . 13. , aut adhaerente . neither be it any subjection , though they give somewhat in sign of superiority castal . de imp . q . 109. . neither be the carthaginian and macedonian subject to the roman , though they do pay unto they a yearly tribute . but if a league be once contract , it bind very strong and effectual ; and that certain ministery or dutiful respect be by reason of such league due and demandable , may evident appear by the league contract betwixt the roman and the lacedaemonian , wherein it be express , that they shall live accord to their own law , and shall not confer any thing in name of tribute , save only certain friendly ministery , and office Strabo lib . • . . and for the further proof of the straitness and strength of this public bond , this form of league be find engrave in a ancient stone : batari fratres , and amici populi Romani dio lib . 69. : so that Bodinus be not to be hearken unto , who think that by friendship or league contract betwixt nation , no aid be due unless it be express mention body . lib . 5. the rep . c . vlt . : for Baldus teach he otherwise , that there be one body of two city or two common weal , by reason of friendship conclude : Bal. con . 29 but for the better understanding hereof it be to be note that there be two kind of society or public friendship : one which be term nonlatinalphabet , the other which be name nonlatinalphabet . Suid. Thucyd. 1. Rhod. 11. a . le . 6. nonlatinalphabet , be when the party contract league be by force of the league to have the same friend , and the same enemy , which manner of society be much use by the roman : the other be but as it be the moiety or half part of this , as namely when one of the league be to help the other when he suffer injury : but not when he offer injury for it be a good rule in the civil law , rei turpis societa non intelligitur : l. 5. 7. ubi Bal. pro for . therefore he that covenant to defend a castle or farelet be not bind , if war be raise through his fault , to who he make the covenant : Alexand. 3. con . 114. and Castrensis avouch , that this aught to be understand of a necessary , not of a voluntary war : but this be clear and certain in this case , that he that be bind so to defend , be bind to defend by weapon : deci . li . 59 de . reg . iur . and 3. con . 117. and he that be bind in such case personal to help a other be much more bind to help he with money Alci . 3. consi 2. yet that must be think to be only then require at his hand , when the other can not provide for his own necessity . now it be to be consider , whether either of the party may depart from the league . and i think upon just occasion such a departure may be make : Faedus non violatur si ab eo disceditur ob rationem iustam l. 14. 15 16. pro soc . : but this must not be for a light cause : for light cause be always arise : and all contract will be most weak , if for a small and worthless cause it shall be break or not regard . but a prince may safe depart from the league if some part of the league be break by the other party : Cagnol . l. 41. Cod. de . trans . and league as all other contract be indiuidua deci . Cons. 265. cep . 455. 461. : for there be always exception understand in every league as these for example ; nisi causa superueniat : nisi culpa accesserit eius cvi promissio ista fit , and pactio foederis : rebus sic stantibus . thus we have speak of war and peace general , now it remain to discourse of the particular circumstance of these two principal point and moment of a common weal .
